posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked:
So I’ve been planning my Christmas dinner and wines today and wondering what everyone else is thinking?

We’re actually doing our big Christmas wine dinner on Christmas Eve and then going out with the family for Thai food on Christmas Day.

Thinking about:

'85 DP with Astrea caviar
'14 Bouchard Monty with a seafood starter TBD
'00 Azelia San Rocco with gorgonzola stuffed beef tenderloin roast
'01 D’Yquem for dessert (with a side of Pumpkin Pie if you must)

posts.name: Scott Watkins
posts.cooked: Bouchard baby Jesus of course.

posts.name: Steve Costigan
posts.cooked:
Just four of us so:
2011 Vilmart Coeur du Cuvee
2011 Cayuse Bionic Frog
and killer Manhattans while cooking beef tenderloin, madeira/mushroom sauce and dauphinois potatoes

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked:
Steve_Costigan:

Just four of us

Oh well my 4 bottles are just for the two of us.

posts.name: Steve Costigan
posts.cooked: With fellow winos it would be four bottles for four but this Christmas there’ll be lighter drinkers.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Difficult this year, as we are very happily expecting another child next year, so I’ll be the only one drinking, which limits the number of bottles somewhat. Thinking of 1964 Bollinger R.D. followed by 1985 Cheval Blanc with roast capon, but might go for a 1993 Truchot Charmes-Chambertin instead.

posts.name: Lasse Knudsen
posts.cooked:
I will be the only one that really cares about wine.

So a 2015 Chateau des Tours CDR and a 2015 Vincent Charlot le Fruit de ma Passion (Champagne).

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
William_Kelley:

Difficult this year, as we are very happily expecting another child next year, so I’ll be the only one drinking, which limits the number of bottles somewhat. Thinking of 1964 Bollinger R.D. followed by 1985 Cheval Blanc with roast capon, but might go for a 1993 Truchot Charmes-Chambertin instead

Per usual…William wins

And a huge congrats that you’re expecting again!! Two kids is amazing…although im not gonna lie…it is SIGNIFICANTLY harder than 1, ha! But for sure well worth it

posts.name: Chris V a n d e r m a r e l
posts.cooked: Congrats William!
posts.display_username: Chris V a n d e r m a r e l

posts.name: Ed Steinway
posts.cooked: Congrats to you and your wife, William.

posts.name: Jonathan Jaggers
posts.cooked: '15 Peter Michael Les Pavots to go with beef tenderloin

posts.name: Adam Wittwer
posts.cooked: Congratulations! Very exciting.

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked:
Rich_Brown:
William_Kelley:

Difficult this year, as we are very happily expecting another child next year, so I’ll be the only one drinking, which limits the number of bottles somewhat. Thinking of 1964 Bollinger R.D. followed by 1985 Cheval Blanc with roast capon, but might go for a 1993 Truchot Charmes-Chambertin instead

Per usual…William wins

And a huge congrats that you’re expecting again!! Two kids is amazing…although im not gonna lie…it is SIGNIFICANTLY harder than 1, ha! But for sure well worth it

Pretty sure this will be three for William - I expect he’s training someone to take over one of his portfolios

posts.name: Pearce Barringer
posts.cooked:
2006 Chateau de Beaucastel Chateauneuf-du-Pape
2019 Raul Perez Ultreia La Claudina
NV Laherte Freres BdB
NV Matthiasson Sweet Vermouth #6

We had the 2005 Clos Mogador Prioriat for thanksgiving, so it’ll be interesting to see what the family thinks of another Grenache heavy blend. I think they’ll like the CDP as it should be more ripe than the Mogador.

posts.name: Dav3_Dyr0ff
posts.cooked:
Christmas dinner means the kids, even if it doesn’t mean Christmas Day itself, and that means a magnum. 1998 Pegau this year, and of course we’ll have plenty of 750s for as many evenings as we can get the kids to hang out with us…

Flannery prime rib on order, too…

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Thanks, everyone! Yes, it’s the third for us…!

posts.name: T 0 m Altmayer
posts.cooked:
‘06 Taittinger Comtes while watching movies and doing puzzles
‘18 A Tribute to Grace Shake Ridge Ranch with appetizers
‘16 William and Mary Proprietary with the lasagne (always tough to pair)

posts.name: Steve Gautier
posts.cooked: Congratulations William!

posts.name: Matthew King
posts.cooked:
Rich_Brown:

And a huge congrats that you’re expecting again!! Two kids is amazing…although im not gonna lie…it is SIGNIFICANTLY harder than 1, ha! But for sure well worth it

I tell everyone worrying about work involved with second kid:

One takes all your free time, so what’s another?

Kids are like a mutual fund — the more units you have, the more you diversify your risk!

posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked:
Congrats WK.

Have Xmas with my folks this year and my old man loves BDX, so will pull whatever he wants from below.

2008 Pichon Lalande
2005 Pichon Baron
2003 Pichon Baron
2005 Pontet Canet
2003 Pontet Canet
2005 Leoville Barton
2005 Lascombes
2000 LLC
2000 L’Evangile

Have some straggler 1961 Croizet Bages as a chemistry experiment too.
